introduction: In Andhra Pradesh, the Marriage Certificate is issued by the Registration and Stamps Department (IGRS) or via the MeeSeva Portal. It officially documents the marital status of a couple, providing legal protection and acting as a requisite for other government applications.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Typically completed on the same day as the physical verification appointment at SRO.
  • Application Fee: Around ₹100-₹200 depending on the Act and MeeSeva service charges.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Photographs, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • SSC Memo
  • Birth Certificate
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Electricity Bill
Evidence of Marriage
  • Marriage invitation card
  • Photographs of the wedding ceremony
Photographs & Witnesses
  • 3 Passport-size photographs of bride and groom
  • Three witnesses with their identity cards (Aadhaar)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply for Marriage Certificate Online

Using the MeeSeva or IGRS portal simplifies the application and slot booking process for the Sub-Registrar visit.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to ap.meeseva.gov.in or the IGRS AP portal at registration.ap.gov.in.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "New Registration", enter your Aadhaar-linked mobile number and verify via OTP. Existing users can log in directly.
  3. Select Marriage Registration Service
    Navigate to Registration & Stamps → "Marriage Registration". Choose the applicable Act (Hindu Marriage Act or Special Marriage Act).
  4. Fill the Application Form
    Enter full names (as per Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, religion, Aadhaar numbers of both spouses, and complete details of all three witnesses.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned JPG/PDF copies (max 200 KB each) of SSC memo/Birth Certificate, Aadhaar, wedding photos, and invitation card.
  6. Pay MeeSeva Fee & Book SRO Slot
    Pay the service charge (~₹35–₹200) online and book your appointment at the nearest Sub-Registrar Office (SRO). An SMS confirmation will be sent.
  7. Attend SRO on Appointment Date
    Both spouses and all three witnesses must appear at the SRO. Carry all originals; the Revenue Inspector verifies and registers the marriage on the spot.
Download Certificate: After registration, download the digitally signed certificate from MeeSeva portal using your application ID.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at MeeSeva Centre / Sub-Registrar Office

Visit any MeeSeva Centre or the local Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) to apply without internet. Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (2nd Saturdays & holidays excluded).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate Nearest MeeSeva / SRO
    Find your nearest centre via ap.meeseva.gov.in → 'Centre Locator'. Alternatively, visit the Sub-Registrar Office of the mandal where the marriage was solemnized.
  2. Collect the Application Form
    Ask the MeeSeva operator or SRO counter for the Marriage Registration Application Form. A nominal service charge of ~₹35 applies at MeeSeva centres.
  3. Fill the Form in BLOCK LETTERS
    Use black ink. Enter: full names (as per Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, Aadhaar numbers of both spouses, and complete details of three witnesses.
  4. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: SSC memo/Birth Certificate, Aadhaar, address proof, wedding invitation card, 3 passport-size photos each of bride and groom, and 3 witness ID proofs. Bring originals for counter verification.
  5. Signatures from All Parties
    The bride, groom, and all three witnesses must sign the form in the presence of the SRO officer.
  6. Submit & Pay Fee
    Submit the complete form and document set. Pay ₹100–₹200 at the counter. Collect the Acknowledgement Receipt with application number.
  7. Collect Certificate
    Certificate is issued same day at the SRO after verification, or SMS notification is sent. Download later from MeeSeva portal.
Processing Time: Same day at SRO. For grievances call AP Civil Supplies Helpline: 1800-425-2977.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Verify and Sign

Both partners and witnesses must visit the SRO at the booked offline appointment time. The Sub-Registrar verifies the originals and registers the marriage.

Certificate Issuance

The digital Marriage Certificate can subsequently be downloaded from the MeeSeva or IGRS portal using your application ID.
